discovery.run_summary: Strong risk-on tape led by semiconductors (SMH +5.76%, TSM +6.94%) and broad tech (XLK +3%), with small caps participating (IWM +2%). Defensive sectors (XLV, XLE, XLF) lag and energy weak on Strait of Hormuz reopening. Most mid-cap movers today look like sector beta rather than idiosyncratic news — semi names like ONTO, VECO, AEIS are riding the TSM/SMH wave with no company-specific 8-Ks.

notable_index_moves: SMH +5.76% with TSM +6.94% — semiconductor leadership pulling tech
XLE -1.65% on Iran/Hormuz sea lane reopening reducing oil risk premium
XLF -0.89% diverging from broader risk-on, possibly rate-sensitive
VIX +2.3% rising alongside SPY +1% — unusual; suggests skew/hedging demand into rally

discoveries.name: Pool Corporation
discoveries.setup: Low-volatility distributor with massive volume spike on no visible catalyst
discoveries.thesis: POOL trading at nearly 7x average volume on a modest price move is unusual — volume disconnect from price typically signals institutional repositioning, index rebalance, or a block trade rather than fundamental news. No 8-K, no earnings window flagged. Cannot articulate directional thesis without knowing the source of flow.
discoveries.what_confirms: An 8-K, analyst day, or guidance update appearing in next 24-48 hours; or confirmation of index inclusion/rebalance
discoveries.what_kills: Volume turns out to be a single block cross with no follow-through tomorrow
discoveries.what_to_learn: Volume multiple >5x with modest price move is a stronger 'something is happening' signal than price alone — but direction requires the catalyst before committing
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no news, no earnings proximity flagged
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals empty; volume_multiple 6.79x is the only anomaly
research_pointers: Check for index rebalance announcements (S&P/Russell) effective near today
Search for analyst day, dealer conference, or pool industry data release
Look for options activity — large call/put sweeps could explain the flow

discoveries.name: JetBlue Airways Corporation
discoveries.setup: Beaten-down airline ripping on macro/oil tailwind with no company news
discoveries.thesis: Double-digit move on a small-cap airline with no 8-K likely tracks the Strait of Hormuz reopening and the resulting oil weakness (XLE -1.65%) — JBLU is the highest-beta US carrier on jet fuel, so it amplifies any oil-down narrative. But moves of this magnitude on macro alone, in a structurally challenged carrier with weak balance sheet, tend to fade once the energy narrative stabilizes. Bear-side bias on fade.
discoveries.what_confirms: Oil reverses higher and JBLU gives back most of the move within 2-3 sessions; no follow-on company announcement
discoveries.what_kills: An actual JBLU-specific catalyst emerges (merger talk, route deal, guidance raise) — would reframe as fundamental rather than macro sympathy
discoveries.what_to_learn: Low-priced, high-short-interest names amplify macro-sympathy moves disproportionately; fade setups are cleaner when the underlying catalyst (here, oil) is itself event-driven rather than trend
discoveries.catalyst: Strait of Hormuz oil tanker traffic resumption pushing oil lower; JBLU as highest fuel-sensitivity carrier
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: XLE down 1.65% on Hormuz news; JBLU has no 8-K, so move is macro-sympathy with leverage
research_pointers: Check short interest and borrow rates on JBLU — squeeze component likely amplifying
Track jet fuel crack spreads vs. JBLU response over coming days
Compare JBLU move to LUV, AAL, UAL — if JBLU outsized vs peers, fade-thesis stronger

discoveries.name: Watsco, Inc.
discoveries.setup: HVAC distributor rallying with housing/building product sympathy
discoveries.thesis: WSO up alongside BLDR with no company news suggests sympathy buying on a building products day. Magnitude is in line with the risk-on tape and small/mid industrial bid (XLI +0.73%, IWM +2%). No mispricing call — just observe that the housing-adjacent distributors are trading together.
discoveries.what_confirms: BLDR, HD, LOW, FAST all up similarly — confirms basket trade
discoveries.what_kills: WSO-specific 8-K appears showing M&A or guide cut/raise
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals; co-movement with BLDR suggests sector sympathy
research_pointers: Cross-check housing starts or homebuilder sentiment data today
Watch for upcoming HVAC channel data points

discoveries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
discoveries.thesis: ONTO move is fully explained by SMH +5.76% and TSM +6.94%. Semicap equipment names (VECO, AEIS, ONTO) all up similarly — beta to the leader, magnitude appropriate. No idiosyncratic catalyst, no mispricing.
discoveries.what_confirms: Other semicap peers (KLAC, LRCX, AMAT) showing similar moves
discoveries.what_kills: Onto-specific guidance or order news appears — would reclassify as fundamental
discoveries.catalyst: Semiconductor sector rally led by TSM
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: SMH +5.76%, TSM +6.94%, multiple semicap peers (VECO, AEIS) moving similarly with no 8-Ks
research_pointers: Check TSM news/guidance update that drove SMH
Track semicap equipment basket — likely all moving together

discoveries.name: Exelixis, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Healthcare/biotech weakness on Germany drug pricing tariff probe
discoveries.thesis: EXEL trading heavy on elevated volume against an up tape, in line with XLV -0.87% weakness. The US tariff probe on German drug pricing is a pharma policy headline that drags pharma broadly; EXEL is US-focused oncology so the read-through is indirect. Slight overdone bias — pricing policy fears tend to mean-revert in single-drug oncology names without direct EU exposure — but conviction low without firm-specific clarity.
discoveries.what_confirms: Other US oncology biotechs recover next session while broader pharma stays weak; EXEL bounces on no news
discoveries.what_kills: An EXEL-specific negative readout, FDA action, or competitive trial result emerges — would justify the weakness fundamentally
discoveries.catalyst: USTR tariff probe on Germany drug pricing dragging pharma sector
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: XLV -0.87% diverging from risk-on tape; EXEL volume 2.6x avg with no company 8-K suggests sector-driven flow
research_pointers: Verify no EXEL-specific clinical or regulatory news today
Compare EXEL move to other US-focused oncology mid-caps (e.g., HALO, NBIX)
Check Cabometyx EU revenue exposure to gauge actual MFN/tariff read-through

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Strait of Hormuz tanker traffic resumption pressuring oil prices lower
TSM rally driving semiconductor sector higher
USTR tariff probe on Germany drug pricing — MFN policy escalation risk

discovery.no_signals_note: Notable observation: VIX up 2.3% while SPY up 1% is mildly unusual and suggests hedging demand into the rally — worth flagging even though it's not actionable. Most mid-cap movers today are explained by sector beta (semis, building products, airlines/oil) rather than idiosyncratic catalysts; POOL is the one genuine puzzle worth tracking for a delayed catalyst.

ai_announcements.headline: US government forced Anthropic to pull Fable 5 and Mythos 5 over national security concerns
ai_announcements.summary: Government ban on Anthropic's two newest models after Amazon researchers found guardrail bypass. Coverage suggests the ban may be helping Anthropic brand-wise and that the same jailbreaks exist in other models. None of today's moving stocks are direct Anthropic counterparties in any obvious way.

ai_announcements.headline: Reliance/Ambani pushing AI into telecom for 500M+ users
ai_announcements.summary: India telecom AI integration. No US-listed stocks in today's mover list have direct exposure.

ai_announcements.headline: Elastic to buy Deductive AI for up to $85M
ai_announcements.summary: Elastic acquiring AI debugging startup. ESTC not in mover list.

ai_announcements.headline: ASML export controversy - US claims top tool in China, ASML denies
ai_announcements.summary: Semicap headline. Several semicap names (VECO, ONTO, AEIS) are up materially today on heavy/elevated volume, but moves look more like broad semicap/risk-on rally than direct reaction to this ASML story (which is mildly negative for ASML, neutral-to-positive for non-ASML semicap competitors only at the margin).

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: ONTO is metrology/inspection, not lithography - not a direct ASML substitute. Move is more likely sector beta / AI capex enthusiasm than ASML-specific.
research_pointers: Check SOX/SMH performance today to confirm sector beta vs idiosyncratic
Check if any AI capex/hyperscaler guidance hit today
ONTO advanced packaging exposure to HBM/AI

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Veeco's laser annealing and MOCVD tools benefit from AI/advanced node capex regardless of ASML China story.
research_pointers: Check broader semicap tape today
Any sell-side notes on advanced packaging beneficiaries

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Power conversion for semicap and data center - AI tailwind story independent of ASML headline.
research_pointers: Data center power exposure mix
Semicap customer concentration

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Most large movers today (POOL +2.4% on 6.8x volume, JBLU +10.7%, VCYT, BEAM, ALGN, BLDR, WSO) have no plausible link to today's AI news flow. POOL's volume spike is notable but unrelated to AI - likely index/M&A/short squeeze rumor; flag for separate investigation. The Anthropic Fable 5/Mythos 5 ban is the day's biggest AI story but has no clean public-stock read-through in this mover set (Amazon not in list). Quiet night for the AI-selloff thesis Michael is hunting.
